ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Continued To Sep-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Thompson appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

Mr. Thompson stated that one of the Beneficiaries has medical and physical issues and has agreed to sign a waiver of accounting, but has not returned the waiver, that the Beneficiary hired an Attorney, but that Attorney has not done anything and the Attorney indicated that the Petitioner's Sister Suzanne Ward wants to buy out the Petitioner (his Client). Mr. Thompson also stated that the Executor wants to sell the house to her Sister with a note back, but they can't seem to get documents signed, that he thought it was going to a simple estate, raised the issue of the Court closing out the estate without a final accounting, and stated that Suzanne's Husband and Son live in the house and it's not an easy case. The Court informed Mr. Thompson that the petition is not properly captioned, that they are also requesting fees, costs and a reserve and that it needs to be reflected in the caption of the petition. The Court also stated that it may be a proper time to follow up with Ms. Ward's Attorney and try to put together a full settlement of the case, and to correct the caption. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Accounting is continued to September 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
